preamble: The reduction of the diphthong [au] into a labiovelar glide [w] represents a loss of syllabicity in the first element, often leading to consonant clusters or complex onsets.

examples.note: Shift of the [u] element to a glide in various environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
